skill.name: wiki-onboarding
skill.description: Creates principal-level and newcomer onboarding guides for a codebase, with architecture context, setup steps, diagrams, and source citations.
skill.summary: Creates paired codebase onboarding guides for senior reviewers and new contributors.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are markdown text and documentation prompts, not executable code. The only confirmed risk is low-risk repository inspection because the skill asks the assistant to scan files and cite source locations when creating onboarding guides.

low_findings.title: Repository File Inspection Required
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to scan repository build files and ground generated claims in source citations. This is expected for documentation generation, but it requires filesystem access to project files and should be used only in repositories the user is authorized to inspect.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require scanning repository files and citing actual code locations. This is a legitimate documentation behavior, but it creates a real filesystem access consideration.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections appear in markdown inline code for command names, file names, and citation formatting. No executable script, shell invocation, interpolation, or command runner is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown documentation, not executable Ruby or shell code. There is no code path that could execute these strings as commands.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary prose in the skill description and guide outline. No hashing, encryption, signing, password handling, or cryptographic API usage appears in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched locations contain documentation wording only. I found no evidence of cryptographic logic or weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Findings Are Documentation Context
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts refer to architecture maps, diagrams, common pitfalls, and workflow guidance. These requests are consistent with an onboarding documentation skill and do not show covert host or network discovery intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe document sections and diagrams for codebase onboarding. No evidence found of hidden system enumeration, persistence, exfiltration, or malicious discovery.

content.value_statement: New engineers need clear architecture context and practical setup steps. This skill creates paired onboarding guides with source citations and diagrams.
actual_capabilities: Creates a principal-level architecture onboarding guide for senior engineers.
Creates a zero-to-hero contributor guide for new developers.
Detects the main project language from common build and package files.
Requires source citations for claims about files, flows, and architecture.
Requests Mermaid diagrams for architecture, data flow, dependencies, and workflows.
limitations: It depends on access to the repository files that need documentation.
It can miss design history that is not present in code, comments, or docs.
It does not create or run a documentation site by itself.
It may need reviewer input for team-specific processes and contacts.

use_cases.title: Prepare Senior Engineer Onboarding
use_cases.description: Create an architecture guide that explains system philosophy, abstractions, trade-offs, data flow, and operational concerns.

use_cases.title: Help First-Time Contributors Start
use_cases.description: Create a practical walkthrough with setup steps, project structure, testing guidance, and a first-task path.

use_cases.title: Document a Legacy Codebase
use_cases.description: Turn existing source files into cited onboarding material that separates architecture context from daily development workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Onboarding Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete onboarding package.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create onboarding documentation for this repository using the wiki-onboarding skill. Include both the architecture guide and the contributor guide.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on New Contributors
prompt_templates.scenario: Contributor-focused documentation for a team or open source project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the zero-to-hero contributor guide for this repository. Emphasize setup, first task, testing, debugging, and common pitfalls.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the principal-level onboarding guide for this repository. Emphasize design principles, key abstractions, data flow, failure modes, and trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Create Cited Migration Onboarding
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced documentation for a codebase handoff or modernization effort.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create both onboarding guides for this repository. Highlight technical debt, dependency rationale, operational concerns, and migration risks with file citations.

output_examples.input: Create onboarding docs for a TypeScript service.
output_examples.output: A senior architecture guide with Mermaid diagrams, source citations, dependency rationale, data flow notes, and security model coverage.

output_examples.input: Help new contributors understand this Python project.
output_examples.output: A contributor guide with prerequisites, setup steps, project structure notes, testing guidance, debugging tips, and a quick reference card.

output_examples.input: Document this legacy repository for a handoff.
output_examples.output: Two complementary guides that explain why the system is built this way and how a new engineer can make a first change.

best_practices: Run it from the repository root so language detection and citations are accurate.
Review generated setup commands before sharing them with new contributors.
Add missing team context, ownership details, and support channels after generation.
anti_patterns: Do not use it on repositories that contain secrets without first applying access controls.
Do not publish generated claims without checking the cited source lines.
Do not replace human architecture review with generated documentation alone.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates two onboarding guides: one for architecture depth and one for practical contributor setup.

faq.question: Does it read the repository?
faq.answer: Yes. It needs repository files to detect language, understand structure, and cite source locations.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: The skill file does not contain executable code. It asks for copy-pasteable commands in the generated documentation.

faq.question: Can it generate diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for Mermaid diagrams that cover architecture, data flow, dependencies, and workflows.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: It is useful for maintainers, technical leads, and teams onboarding engineers into an existing codebase.

faq.question: What should reviewers verify?
faq.answer: Reviewers should verify citations, setup commands, architecture claims, and any team-specific process details.
